title: Minimum viable village restart board
summary: A worst-case routine for prioritizing water, sanitation, food, shelter, records, safety, learning, and repair after total disruption.
content: Minimum viable village restart board is designed for conditions where normal services, internet, transport, or outside help may be delayed. The practical goal is prioritizing water, sanitation, food, shelter, records, safety, learning, and repair after total disruption. Keep the routine visible, assign one calm recorder, protect vulnerable people first, and change the plan only when conditions or official instructions clearly change.
materials: Notebook or board. Context: “Minimum viable village restart board”.
Marker or pencil. Context: “Minimum viable village restart board”.
Tape or labels. Context: “Minimum viable village restart board”.
Local map or contact list — for «Minimum viable village restart board»
steps: Write the exact trigger for starting minimum viable village restart board.
List people, supplies, hazards, and safe locations before action starts. Apply it specifically within the “Minimum viable village restart board” procedure.
Use the routine to focus on prioritizing water, sanitation, food, shelter, records, safety, learning, and repair after total disruption.
Check vulnerable people first and document unmet needs. Apply it specifically within the “Minimum viable village restart board” procedure.
Review the plan at fixed times or when official instructions change. Apply it specifically within the “Minimum viable village restart board” procedure.
warnings: Follow local authorities and qualified responders whenever they are available. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Minimum viable village restart board”.
Do not improvise with weapons, explosives, dangerous chemicals, medical dosing, or unsafe building entry. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Minimum viable village restart board”.
Do not let curiosity, filming, rumors, or pride replace distance, shelter, evacuation, hygiene, and records. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Minimum viable village restart board”.
common_mistakes: Starting too late because the first signs were ignored while carrying out “Minimum viable village restart board”
Letting many people make conflicting decisions at the same time while carrying out “Minimum viable village restart board”
Using memory instead of a visible written record while carrying out “Minimum viable village restart board”
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Minimum viable village restart board with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Minimum viable village restart board if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Minimum viable village restart board and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Minimum viable village restart board or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Minimum viable village restart board are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.
The routine ignores water, hygiene, food contact, and waste boundaries and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Minimum viable village restart board”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Minimum viable village restart board as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Minimum viable village restart board when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Minimum viable village restart board.
short_term: For the first hours of Minimum viable village restart board, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Minimum viable village restart board remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Minimum viable village restart board fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Minimum viable village restart board to the real environment: check water, hygiene, food contact, and waste boundaries, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
